paper_title: Inferring locomotor behaviours in Miocene New World monkeys using finite element analysis, geometric morphometrics and machine-learning classification techniques applied to talar morphology

methods.method_name: Machine Learning Model Parameter Tuning and Fossil Classification
methods.description: This method describes the process of optimizing machine learning model parameters using the 'caret' package's grid search capabilities (automatic and manual) on biomechanical and morphometric datasets. The goal is to identify the best performing model, which is then used to classify a fossil sample by calculating class probabilities for different locomotor categories.

steps.description: We first started with an automatic grid search by setting the 'tuneLength' option to indicate the number of different values to try for each algorithm parameter (we initially set this parameter to 10 in all the tested models). This only supports integer algorithm parameters, thus providing a quick first guess as to what values to try and which models are more promising.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform automatic grid search for ML model parameter tuning using caret package
outputs: Identification of promising models and parameter ranges

steps.description: Then the models that were the most accurate for each one of the datasets (i.e., biomechanical and morphometric data) were further tuned by setting a manual grid search.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify best performing ML models from initial tuning results
inputs: Initial model performance results from Step 1
outputs: List of promising ML models for each dataset

steps.description: In the grid, each algorithm parameter was specified as a vector of possible values. These vectors were combined to define all the possible combinations to try in order to further improve the performance of the model.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform manual grid search for selected ML model parameter tuning using caret package
outputs: Detailed model performance results for various parameter combinations

steps.description: Then using the best final model the fossil sample was classified...
steps.searchable_summary: Select the overall best performing ML model after comprehensive tuning
inputs: Detailed model performance results from Step 3

steps.description: Then using the best final model the fossil sample was classified into the different locomotor categories by computing the class probabilities of belonging to each one of the categories.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify a fossil sample using the best trained ML model and compute class probabilities
outputs: Classification results for the fossil sample
